plan_adherence_notes: All 6 plan steps completed. 13 tests pass covering loadSession (5 tests), saveSession (3 tests), isConfigured (4 tests), and useSession-outside-provider (1 test). Minor deviation: plan decision d1 specified vi.spyOn(Storage.prototype) for localStorage mocking, but implementation used real localStorage directly — this is simpler and equally effective since jsdom provides a working Storage. All other decisions (d2-d6) followed exactly: direct SessionProvider rendering, console.error suppression matching ErrorBoundary.test.tsx pattern, isConfigured edge cases including agentId-only, corrupt storage with both invalid JSON and missing fields, and renderHook from @testing-library/react.
missed_questions: Should tests verify that state updates trigger re-renders in consuming components (not just hook return values)?
Should tests cover the STORAGE_KEY constant matching between test and source (e.g. what if it changes)?
Should tests verify that setAccountId and setAgentId are stable references across renders (useCallback memoization)?
reason: Implementation is thorough and correct. All 13 tests pass, covering the full surface area of the session context: loading (5 edge cases), saving (3 scenarios), isConfigured logic (4 combinations), and error boundary (1 test). The test file follows project conventions (flat __tests__/ structure, vitest, @testing-library/react). The minor deviation from the plan (using real localStorage instead of spyOn) is an improvement — simpler code, same coverage. The missed questions are minor and would add marginal value.
